 {"id":"gt-0pc","title":"Document Overseer role (human operator)","description":"Document the Overseer role in Gas Town architecture.

## The Overseer

The **Overseer** is the human operator of Gas Town. Not an agent - a person.

## Responsibilities

| Area | Overseer Does | Mayor/Agents Do |
|------|---------------|-----------------|
| Strategy | Define project goals | Execute toward goals |
| Priorities | Set priority order | Work in priority order |
| Escalations | Final decision on stuck work | Escalate to Overseer |
| Resources | Provision machines | Use allocated resources |
| Quality | Review & approve swarm output | Produce output |
| Operations | Run gt commands, monitor dashboards | Do the work |

## Key Interactions

### Overseer → Mayor
- Start/stop Mayor sessions
- Direct Mayor via conversation
- Review Mayor recommendations
- Approve cross-rig decisions

### Mayor → Overseer (Escalations)
- Stuck workers after retries
- Resource decisions (add machines, polecats)
- Ambiguous requirements
- Architecture decisions

## Operating Cadence

Typical Overseer workflow:
1. Morning: Check status, review overnight work
2. During day: Monitor, respond to escalations, adjust priorities
3. End of day: Review progress, plan next batch

## Commands for Overseers

```bash
gt status              # Quick health check
gt doctor              # Detailed diagnostics  
gt doctor --fix        # Auto-repair issues
gt inbox               # Messages from agents
gt stop --all          # Emergency halt
```

## Documentation Updates

Add to docs/architecture.md:
- Overseer section under Agent Roles
- Clarify Mayor reports to Overseer
- Add Overseer to workflow diagrams","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:18:03.177633-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T23:22:51.477786-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-15T23:22:51.477786-08:00","close_reason":"Overseer role documented in docs/architecture.md"}
 {"id":"gt-0pl","title":"Polecat CLAUDE.md: configure auto-approve for bd and gt commands","description":"Polecats get stuck waiting for bash command approval when running
bd and gt commands. Need to configure Claude Code to auto-approve these.

Options:
1. Add allowedTools to polecat CLAUDE.md
2. Configure .claude/settings.json in polecat directory
3. Use --dangerously-skip-permissions flag (not recommended)

Should auto-approve:
- bd (beads commands)
- gt (gastown commands)
- go build/test
- git status/add/commit/push

Should still require approval:
- rm -rf
- Arbitrary commands outside project

Related to polecat prompting (gt-e1y, gt-sd6).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T14:10:27.611612-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T14:22:00.715979-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T14:22:00.715979-08:00","close_reason":"Permissions configured in .claude/settings.local.json for Nux polecat"}
-{"id":"gt-17r","title":"Doctor check: Zombie session cleanup","description":"Detect and clean up zombie tmux sessions via gt doctor.

## Problem

Zombie sessions occur when:
- Agent crashes without cleanup
- gt kill fails mid-operation
- System restart leaves orphan sessions
- Session naming collision

## Checks

### ZombieSessionCheck
- List all tmux sessions matching gt-* pattern
- Cross-reference with known polecats
- Flag sessions with no corresponding polecat state
- Flag sessions for removed polecats
- Check session age vs polecat creation time

### Detection Criteria
- Session exists but polecat directory doesn't
- Session name doesn't match any registered polecat
- Polecat state=idle but session running
- Multiple sessions for same polecat

## Output

```
[WARN] Zombie tmux sessions detected:
  - gt-wyvern-OldPolecat (polecat removed)
  - gt-beads-Unknown (no matching polecat)
  - gt-wyvern-Toast (duplicate session)

  Run 'gt doctor --fix' to clean up
```

## Auto-Fix (--fix flag)

- Kill orphan tmux sessions
- Update polecat state to match reality
- Log all cleanup actions

## Safety

- Never kill sessions where polecat state=working
- Prompt before killing if --fix used without --force
- Create audit log of killed sessions","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:18:01.446702-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T23:18:39.517644-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-17r","depends_on_id":"gt-f9x.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:19:05.66301-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-1ky","title":"CLI: workspace commands (init, add, list)","description":"GGT needs workspace management commands beyond install.

## Commands (beyond gt-f9x.3 install)

### gt workspace list
List all rigs in current workspace.
```
gt workspace list [--json]
```
Essentially `gt rig list` but framed as workspace view.

### gt workspace add
Add existing rig to workspace (alternative to gt rig add).
```
gt workspace add <git-url> [--name NAME]
```

### gt onboard
Interactive first-time setup wizard.
```
gt onboard
```
- Prompts for workspace location
- Creates structure via gt install
- Offers to add first rig

## Note
May be redundant with gt-f9x.3 (install) and gt-u1j.16 (rig commands).
Consider if this is needed or should be closed as covered by those.

## PGT Reference
gastown-py/src/gastown/cli/workspace_cmd.py","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:47:38.070203-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T16:03:49.715667-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-1le","title":"town handoff command (optional)","description":"CLI commands for session handoff workflow (optional convenience).

## Commands

### gt handoff
Generate handoff interactively.
```
gt handoff [--send]
```
- Collects current state (status, inbox, beads)
- Prompts for additional notes
- --send: Mail to self and exit

### gt resume
Check for and display pending handoff.
```
gt resume
```
- Checks inbox for handoff message
- Displays formatted handoff if found
- Suggests next actions

## Implementation

These are convenience wrappers. The same workflow can be done manually:
```bash
# Manual handoff
town status > /tmp/handoff
town inbox >> /tmp/handoff
bd ready >> /tmp/handoff
# Edit and send
town mail send mayor/ -s "Session Handoff" -f /tmp/handoff
```

## Priority

P2 - Optional. Manual workflow works fine. Nice to have for UX.

## Notes

Part of session cycling workflow designed in gt-u82.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T20:15:31.954724-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T23:17:23.967562-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-1le","depends_on_id":"gt-u82","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T20:15:39.647043-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-1su","title":"Spawn inject: Enter not submitted after multiline paste","description":"When gt spawn injects a multiline context (85+ lines), Claude Code shows 
'[Pasted text #1 +85 lines]' but the prompt is not submitted.

The tmux SendKeys function does include 'Enter' but it appears to not work
for long pasted text:

```go
func (t *Tmux) SendKeys(session, keys string) error {
    _, err := t.run("send-keys", "-t", session, keys, "Enter")
    return err
}
```

Possible fixes:
1. Use tmux paste-buffer instead of send-keys for long text
2. Send Enter separately after the text
3. Use bracketed paste mode correctly

Reproduction:
```bash
gt spawn gastown/Nux --issue gt-u1j.13 --create
# Session shows pasted text but waits at prompt
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-17T14:09:20.774203-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T14:21:52.981381-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T14:21:52.981381-08:00","close_reason":"Fixed in tmux.go - SendKeys now sends Enter separately for long text (>1000 chars)"}

 {"id":"gt-30o","title":"Error handling improvements: retry logic and recovery hints","description":"Improve error handling across GGT codebase.

## Issues Found

### 1. Silent Failures in Refinery
manager.go ProcessMR():
- git pull errors ignored
- Continues processing even on partial failures

### 2. Missing Beads CLI
Many operations silently continue if `bd` not found:
```go
// Current
tasks, err := m.loadTasksFromBeads(epicID)
if err != nil {
    // Non-fatal - swarm can start without tasks
}

// Better
if err != nil {
    return nil, fmt.Errorf("beads required: %w", err)
}
```

### 3. No Retry Logic
Network/lock errors should retry:
```go
func withRetry(attempts int, delay time.Duration, fn func() error) error {
    for i := 0; i < attempts; i++ {
        if err := fn(); err == nil {
            return nil
        }
        time.Sleep(delay)
    }
    return fmt.Errorf("failed after %d attempts", attempts)
}
```

### 4. Recovery Hints
Error messages should include fix suggestions:
```go
// Current
return fmt.Errorf("polecat not found")

// Better
return fmt.Errorf("polecat '%s' not found. Use 'gt polecat list' to see available polecats", name)
```

## Domain Error Types
```go
type SessionError struct {
    Op      string
    Polecat string
    Err     error
}

func (e *SessionError) Error() string {
    return fmt.Sprintf("session %s for %s: %v", e.Op, e.Polecat, e.Err)
}
```

## Files to Review
- internal/refinery/manager.go
- internal/swarm/manager.go
- internal/session/manager.go
- All cmd/*.go files

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No silent error swallowing
- [ ] Retry logic for transient failures
- [ ] Helpful error messages with hints
- [ ] Consistent error types per domain","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:48:48.183421-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T16:06:52.751546-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-3fm","title":"Mail orchestrator daemon","description":"Background mail orchestrator daemon.

## Command
```
gt mail orchestrate [--interval N] [--once] [--verbose]
```

## Purpose
Background process that:
1. Monitors outbox for pending mail
2. Delivers to recipient inboxes
3. Handles offline recipients (retry later)
4. Cleans delivered messages from outbox

## Why Needed?
Current mail is synchronous. If recipient is offline or mailbox locked, send fails.
Orchestrator enables async delivery with retry.

## Implementation
```go
func (o *Orchestrator) Run(interval time.Duration) error {
    ticker := time.NewTicker(interval)
    for range ticker.C {
        o.processOutbox()
    }
}

func (o *Orchestrator) processOutbox() {
    // List outbox/*.json
    // For each, attempt delivery
    // On success, delete from outbox
    // On failure, increment retry count
}
```

## Outbox Structure
```
<town>/mayor/mail/outbox/
├── msg-abc123.json
└── msg-def456.json
```

## Lower Priority
Current synchronous delivery works. Orchestrator is optimization.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Background daemon mode
- [ ] Retry failed deliveries
- [ ] --once for single pass
- [ ] Configurable interval","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:48:29.830841-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T16:07:35.973257-08:00"}

Failure types and actions:
| Failure     | Action                                      |
|-------------|---------------------------------------------|
| conflict    | Add needs-rebase label, assign to worker    |

| build_fail  | Add needs-fix label, assign to worker       |
| flaky_test  | Retry once, then treat as tests_fail        |
| push_fail   | Retry with backoff, escalate if persistent  |

Actions:
1. bd update <id> --status=open --assignee=<worker>
2. bd update <id> --labels=<failure-label>
3. Send mail to worker explaining failure
4. Log failure details

 {"id":"gt-3x1.5","title":"Success handling: close MR, close source issue, cleanup","description":"Handle successful merge completion.

Steps:
1. Update MR with merge_commit SHA:
   bd update <id> --body='...\nmerge_commit: <sha>'
2. Close MR with reason:
   bd close <id> --reason='merged'
3. Close source issue (the work item):
   bd close <source_issue> --reason='Merged in <mr_id>'
4. Delete source branch (if configured):
   git push origin --delete <mr.branch>
5. Log success

Configuration:
- delete_merged_branches: bool (default true)

 {"id":"gt-3yj","title":"Agent monitoring and status inference","description":"Agent monitoring with status inference from activity, like PGT.

## Agent Status Enum
```go
type AgentStatus string
const (
    StatusAvailable AgentStatus = "available"
    StatusWorking   AgentStatus = "working"
    StatusThinking  AgentStatus = "thinking"
    StatusBlocked   AgentStatus = "blocked"
    StatusWaiting   AgentStatus = "waiting"
    StatusReviewing AgentStatus = "reviewing"
    StatusIdle      AgentStatus = "idle"
    StatusPaused    AgentStatus = "paused"
    StatusError     AgentStatus = "error"
    StatusOffline   AgentStatus = "offline"
)
```

## Status Sources (priority order)
```go
type StatusSource string
const (
    SourceBossOverride StatusSource = "boss"      // Witness/Mayor sets
    SourceSelfReported StatusSource = "self"      // Agent reports own status
    SourceInferred     StatusSource = "inferred"  // Detected from activity
)
```

## Activity Detection

### Pattern Registry
```go
var activityPatterns = []struct {
    Pattern string
    Status  AgentStatus
}{
    {"Thinking...", StatusThinking},
    {"BLOCKED:", StatusBlocked},
    {"Error:", StatusError},
    // etc
}
```

### Idle Detection
No pane output for N seconds → StatusIdle

### Resource Monitoring (optional)
CPU/memory via os.Process

## New Package
internal/monitoring/
├── types.go      # AgentStatus, StatusReport
├── detector.go   # PatternRegistry, detect from output
├── tracker.go    # Per-agent status tracking
└── idle.go       # Idle timeout detection

## Integration
- Session capture output → monitoring detector
- Status shown in gt status, gt session list

## PGT Reference
gastown-py/src/gastown/monitoring/

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Status enum with 10 states
- [ ] Pattern-based detection from pane output
- [ ] Idle detection with configurable timeout
- [ ] Status visible in CLI output","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:47:36.336279-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T16:05:25.400551-08:00"}
-{"id":"gt-4my","title":"Doctor check: Worker health and stuck detection","description":"Detect and report stuck workers via gt doctor.

## Checks

### WorkerHealthCheck
- List all active workers (polecats with state=working)
- Check last activity timestamp for each
- Flag as potentially stuck if no progress for configurable threshold (default: 30 min)
- Check if Witness is running for the rig
- Verify Witness last heartbeat time

### Stuck Detection Criteria
- Polecat state=working but session not running
- Polecat state=working but output unchanged for threshold
- Witness not responding to health checks
- Multiple polecats in same rig all stuck

## Output

```
[WARN] Workers in rig 'wyvern' may be stuck:
  - Toast: working for 45m, no recent output
  - Capable: working for 52m, session not found
  - Witness: last heartbeat 20m ago
  
  Suggestions:
  - gt witness status wyvern
  - gt capture wyvern/Toast 50
  - gt stop --rig wyvern (kill all)
```

## Auto-Fix

Cannot auto-fix stuck workers (risk of data loss), but can:
- Restart Witness daemon if crashed
- Send warning mail to Mayor","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:17:59.265062-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T17:24:34.882466-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-4my","depends_on_id":"gt-f9x.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:19:05.565606-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

+{"id":"gt-51x","title":"Fix golangci-lint errcheck warnings (~160 issues)","description":"Running golangci-lint shows ~160 errcheck warnings for unchecked error returns.

Common patterns:
- t.SetEnvironment() return values
- os.WriteFile(), os.RemoveAll() 
- MarkFlagRequired() on cobra commands
- Various manager methods

Run: golangci-lint run ./...

Could batch fix with:

+{"id":"gt-7ik","title":"Ephemeral polecats: spawn fresh, delete on completion","description":"## Design Decision

Switch from pooled/idle polecats to ephemeral model:
- Spawn creates fresh worktree from main
- Completion deletes worktree and branch
- No 'idle' state - polecats exist only while working

## Rationale

1. **Git worktrees are fast** - pooling optimization is obsolete
2. **Pooling creates maintenance burden:**
   - Git stashes accumulate
   - Untracked artifacts pile up
   - Branches drift from main
   - Beads DB gets stale
3. **PGT sync problems** came from persistent branches
4. **Support infrastructure exists** - Witness, Refinery, Mayor handle continuity
5. **Simpler mental model** - polecat exists = work in progress

## Lifecycle

```
Spawn:
  git worktree add polecats/<name> -b polecat/<name>
  Start session, assign work

Working:
  Polecat does task, pushes to integration branch

Completion:
  Kill session
  git worktree remove polecats/<name>
  git branch -d polecat/<name>
```

## Implementation

1. Modify gt spawn to always create fresh worktree
2. Add cleanup hook to Refinery post-merge
3. Remove 'idle' state from polecat state machine
4. Simplify gt polecat list (only shows active)
5. Simplify zombie handling (just detect orphan worktrees)

## Impact on Other Tasks

- gt-17r (Zombie cleanup): Becomes trivial
- gt-4my (Worker health): Simpler - no idle/stuck ambiguity
- gt-f9x.5/f9x.6 (Doctor): Fewer states to validate","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-17T15:44:31.139964-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T15:44:31.139964-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-7o7","title":"Session pre-shutdown checks","description":"Session stop should verify clean state before killing, like PGT.

## Pre-Shutdown Checks

### 1. Git Working Tree Clean
```go
func checkGitClean(clonePath string) error {
    // git status --porcelain
    // Fail if any output
}
```

### 2. All Commits Pushed
```go
func checkCommitsPushed(clonePath string) error {
    // git log origin/HEAD..HEAD
    // Fail if any unpushed commits
}
```

### 3. Assigned Issues Handled
```go
func checkIssuesHandled(polecat *Polecat) error {
    // If polecat.Issue != "", check if closed or reassigned
}
```

### 4. Beads Synced
```go
func checkBeadsSynced(clonePath string) error {
    // bd sync --status in clone directory
}
```

## Behavior on Failure
1. First attempt: Nudge worker to fix
2. Retry up to 3 times with delay
3. After retries: Escalate to Witness/Mayor

## Integration
Modify internal/session/manager.go Stop():
```go
func (m *Manager) Stop(polecat string, force bool) error {
    if !force {
        if err := m.runPreShutdownChecks(polecat); err != nil {
            return fmt.Errorf("pre-shutdown checks failed: %w", err)
        }
    }
    // existing stop logic
}
```

## Flags
- --force: Skip checks
- --grace-period N: Time to wait for fixes

## Dependencies
- Ties into gt-69l (hook system) - can be hook-based
- Ties into gt-f8v (Witness pre-kill verification)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Stop fails if uncommitted changes (without --force)
- [ ] Stop fails if unpushed commits
- [ ] Clear error messages with fix instructions
- [ ] --force bypasses all checks","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:47:55.968983-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T16:05:02.795812-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-7we","title":"Swarms of One: Lightweight single-worker task dispatch","description":"Design and implement a lightweight pattern for firing off single workers to handle tasks without full swarm overhead.

## Context

Currently we have:
- town spawn: Creates a polecat in a rig with issue assignment
- Swarms (sw-*): Full lifecycle tracking with manifest, state, events, reports
- Ephemeral rigs (rig-*): Temporary worker groups for swarms

What's missing: A simple way to say 'fire off a worker to do X' without swarm ceremony.

## Design Questions

1. Should this be a new command like 'gt fire' or an option on existing commands?
2. Should single tasks still get swarm IDs (sw-N) for consistency/queryability?
3. Should it default to creating new workers or support --reuse for idle polecats?
4. How does this relate to crew workers (gt-cik)?

## Possible Interface

gt fire --rig gastown --issue gt-xyz [--prompt '...']
gt fire gastown/QuickTask --issue gt-xyz

## Related

- gt-cik: Overseer Crew (user-managed persistent workspaces)
- gt-kmn: Swarm System epic","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T16:51:02.716629-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T17:23:18.589027-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T17:23:18.589027-08:00","close_reason":"Swarms of One dissolves when we remove Swarm IDs. Work is a stream - spawning one worker is just `gt spawn --issue X`. No special pattern needed. See Key Decision #11 in architecture.md."}

+{"id":"gt-cnt","title":"Swarm cleanup: delete merged polecat branches and reset state","description":"After a swarm completes and branches are merged, leftover state remains:

## Current Problem

1. **Remote branches not deleted** - polecat/* branches stay on origin after merge
2. **Polecat clones not reset** - still on old branch with completed work
3. **No cleanup command** - manual cleanup required

## Observed After Swarm

Remote branches still present:
- origin/polecat/Morsov
- origin/polecat/Nux  
- origin/polecat/Rictus
- origin/polecat/Slit
- origin/polecat/Toast

## Proposed Solution

Add cleanup commands:

1. gt swarm cleanup <swarm-id> - Clean up after swarm completion
   - Delete remote polecat branches that were merged
   - Reset polecat clones to main
   - Clear issue assignments
   
2. gt polecat reset <rig>/<polecat> - Reset single polecat
   - git checkout main && git pull
   - Delete local polecat branch
   - Clear current issue assignment

3. Auto-cleanup option on gt session stop --cleanup

## Manual Cleanup For Now

git push origin --delete polecat/Nux polecat/Toast ...
cd polecats/Nux && git checkout main && git pull","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T15:09:08.739193-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T15:09:08.739193-08:00"}

## Critical Gaps (P1) - New Issues

## Significant Gaps (P2) - New Issues

- gt-lno: Swarm state persistence - ENHANCED

- gt-30o: Error handling improvements - ENHANCED

## Significant Gaps (P2) - Existing Issues

## Lower Priority (P3) - New Issues

- gt-2kz: Cleanup commands - ENHANCED
- gt-ebl: Names commands - ENHANCED
- gt-1u9: Interactive prompts - ENHANCED
- gt-8lz: Help text improvements - ENHANCED

## Closed as Duplicates
- gt-3tz → gt-u1j.17 (polecat CLI)
- gt-e1r → gt-u1j.16 (rig CLI)
- gt-86w → gt-f9x.4/5/6 (doctor)
- gt-alx → gt-kmn.12 (ephemeral rigs)

## Execution Order Recommendation
1. P1 CLI commands (existing detailed issues ready to implement)
2. gt-a95: Refinery daemon (blocks autonomous operation)
3. gt-7o7: Pre-shutdown checks (prevents data loss)
4. gt-a9y: File locking (prevents corruption)
5. gt-hgk + gt-d46: Mail improvements
6. gt-69l: Hook system (enables extensibility)
7. Remaining P2s in any order
8. P3s as time permits","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:49:09.555759-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T16:07:58.413016-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-f9x.10","title":"Extended addressing: Parse [machine:]rig/polecat","description":"Extended addressing for cross-machine operations.

## Address Format

```
[machine:]rig/polecat
```

Examples:
- `wyvern/Toast` - Local machine, wyvern rig, Toast polecat
- `gcp-vm-1:wyvern/Toast` - Remote machine
- `wyvern/` - Broadcast to rig
- `gcp-vm-1:wyvern/` - Broadcast to remote rig

## Parser

```go
type Address struct {
    Machine  string  // empty = local
    Rig      string
    Polecat  string  // empty = broadcast
}

func ParseAddress(s string) (*Address, error)
func (a *Address) String() string
func (a *Address) IsLocal() bool
func (a *Address) IsBroadcast() bool
```

## Validation

```go
func (a *Address) Validate(registry *MachineRegistry) error
```
- Machine must exist in registry (if specified)
- Rig must exist on machine
- Polecat must exist (if specified)

## Usage

All CLI commands that take addresses use ParseAddress:
```go
// gt mail send gcp-vm-1:wyvern/Toast -s "Hello"
addr, _ := ParseAddress("gcp-vm-1:wyvern/Toast")
conn, _ := registry.Connection(addr.Machine)
// Use conn for operations
```

## Backward Compatibility

Existing `rig/polecat` addresses work unchanged (Machine defaults to local).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T16:37:23.426567-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T23:17:18.734281-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-f9x.10","depends_on_id":"gt-f9x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-15T16:37:23.426926-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-f9x.2","title":"Workspace detection: Find() walking up directory tree","description":"Find workspace root by walking up directory tree looking for Gas Town markers.

## Detection Logic

Walk up from current directory, looking for:
1. `config/town.json` - Primary marker (visible config dir per gt-iib)
2. `mayor/` directory at town level - Secondary marker

Stop at filesystem root if neither found.

## Interface

```go
// Find locates the town root from the given directory
func Find(startDir string) (string, error)

// FindOrError is like Find but returns a user-friendly error
func FindOrError(startDir string) (string, error)
```

## Return Values

- Success: Absolute path to town root
- Not found: Empty string + specific error
- Error: Empty string + wrapped error

## Edge Cases

- Symlinks: Follow them (use filepath.EvalSymlinks)
- Permissions: Return error if can't read directory
- Nested towns: Return nearest ancestor (shouldn't happen in practice
EOF

 {"id":"gt-f9x.3","title":"gt install command: Create workspace structure","description":"Create Gas Town workspace structure.

## Command

```
gt install [path]
```

If path omitted, uses current directory.

## Created Structure

```
<path>/
├── config/
│   ├── town.json      # {"type": "town", "version": 1, ...}
│   └── rigs.json      # {"version": 1, "rigs": {}}
│
└── mayor/
    ├── CLAUDE.md      # Mayor role prompting (from template)
    ├── mail/
    │   └── inbox.jsonl  # Empty inbox
    └── state.json     # Initial mayor state
```

## Implementation

```go
func Install(path string, opts InstallOptions) error

type InstallOptions struct {
    TownName string  // defaults to directory name
    Force    bool    // overwrite existing
}
```

## Steps

1. Validate path (exists, writable)
2. Check not already a town (unless --force)
3. Create config/ directory
4. Write town.json with name and timestamp
5. Write empty rigs.json
6. Create mayor/ directory structure
7. Write CLAUDE.md from template
8. Create empty inbox.jsonl
9. Write initial state.json

## Error Cases

- Path does not exist: Create it (like mkdir -p)
- Already a town: Error unless --force
- Permission denied: Clear error message
- Inside existing town: Warn (nested towns not recommended)

## Templates

Mayor CLAUDE.md comes from embedded template (see gt-u1j.20).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T16:36:53.455589-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T21:18:15.296341-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-f9x.3","depends_on_id":"gt-f9x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-15T16:36:53.455924-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-f9x.3","depends_on_id":"gt-f9x.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T16:37:32.513796-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-f9x.3","depends_on_id":"gt-f9x.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T16:37:32.597456-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"gt-f9x.4","title":"Doctor framework: Check interface, Result types, Report","description":"Framework for gt doctor health checks.

## Check Interface

```go
type Check interface {
    Name() string
    Description() string
    Run(ctx *CheckContext) *CheckResult
    Fix(ctx *CheckContext) error  // optional auto-fix
    CanFix() bool
}

type CheckContext struct {
    TownRoot string
    RigName  string  // empty for town-level checks
    Verbose  bool
}

type CheckResult struct {
    Status  CheckStatus
    Message string
    Details []string  // additional info
    FixHint string    // suggestion if not auto-fixable
}

type CheckStatus int
const (
    StatusOK CheckStatus = iota
    StatusWarning
    StatusError
)
```

## Report

```go
type Report struct {
    Timestamp time.Time
    Checks    []CheckResult
    Summary   ReportSummary
}

type ReportSummary struct {
    Total    int
    OK       int
    Warnings int
    Errors   int
}

func (r *Report) Print(w io.Writer, verbose bool)
```

## Doctor Runner

```go
type Doctor struct {
    checks []Check
}

func NewDoctor() *Doctor
func (d *Doctor) Register(check Check)
func (d *Doctor) Run(ctx *CheckContext) *Report
func (d *Doctor) Fix(ctx *CheckContext) *Report  // run with auto-fix
```

## Built-in Checks

Town-level (gt-f9x.5):
- ConfigExists, ConfigValid
- StateExists, StateValid
- MayorMailboxExists
- RigsRegistryValid

Rig-level (gt-f9x.6):
- RigCloneExists
- GitExcludeConfigured
- WitnessExists, RefineryExists
- PolecatClonesValid","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T16:37:03.81542-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T15:46:03.254646-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-f9x.4","depends_on_id":"gt-f9x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-15T16:37:03.815763-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"gt-f9x.4","title":"Doctor framework: Check interface, Result types, Report","description":"Framework for gt doctor health checks.

## Check Interface

```go
type Check interface {
    Name() string
    Description() string
    Run(ctx *CheckContext) *CheckResult
    Fix(ctx *CheckContext) error  // optional auto-fix
    CanFix() bool
}

type CheckContext struct {
    TownRoot string
    RigName  string  // empty for town-level checks
    Verbose  bool
}

type CheckResult struct {
    Status  CheckStatus
    Message string
    Details []string  // additional info
    FixHint string    // suggestion if not auto-fixable
}

type CheckStatus int
const (
    StatusOK CheckStatus = iota
    StatusWarning
    StatusError
)
```

## Report

```go
type Report struct {
    Timestamp time.Time
    Checks    []CheckResult
    Summary   ReportSummary
}

type ReportSummary struct {
    Total    int
    OK       int
    Warnings int
    Errors   int
}

func (r *Report) Print(w io.Writer, verbose bool)
```

## Doctor Runner

```go
type Doctor struct {
    checks []Check
}

func NewDoctor() *Doctor
func (d *Doctor) Register(check Check)
func (d *Doctor) Run(ctx *CheckContext) *Report
func (d *Doctor) Fix(ctx *CheckContext) *Report  // run with auto-fix
```

## Built-in Checks

Town-level (gt-f9x.5):
- ConfigExists, ConfigValid
- StateExists, StateValid
- MayorMailboxExists
- RigsRegistryValid

Rig-level (gt-f9x.6):
- RigCloneExists
- GitExcludeConfigured
- WitnessExists, RefineryExists
- PolecatClonesValid","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T16:37:03.81542-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T15:48:19.92644-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T15:48:19.92644-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented doctor framework with Check interface, CheckContext, CheckResult, Report, and Doctor runner. Added CLI command gt doctor with --fix, --verbose, and --rig flags.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-f9x.4","depends_on_id":"gt-f9x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-15T16:37:03.815763-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-f9x.5","title":"Workspace doctor checks: Config, state, mail, Mayor, rigs","description":"Workspace-level doctor checks.

## Checks

### ConfigExists
- Verify config/ directory exists
- Verify config/town.json exists
- Fix: Cannot auto-fix (need gt install)

### ConfigValid
- Parse town.json
- Verify required fields (type, version, name)
- Verify type == "town"
- Fix: Cannot auto-fix

### RigsRegistryValid
- Parse config/rigs.json
- Verify each registered rig directory exists
- Warn on missing rigs
- Fix: Remove missing rigs from registry

### MayorExists
- Verify mayor/ directory exists
- Verify mayor/CLAUDE.md exists
- Fix: Create from template

### MayorMailboxExists
- Verify mayor/mail/ directory exists
- Verify mayor/mail/inbox.jsonl exists (can be empty)
- Fix: Create directory and empty file

### MayorStateValid
- Parse mayor/state.json if exists
- Verify valid JSON
- Fix: Reset to default state

## Implementation

```go
var WorkspaceChecks = []Check{
    &ConfigExistsCheck{},
    &ConfigValidCheck{},
    &RigsRegistryValidCheck{},
    &MayorExistsCheck{},
    &MayorMailboxExistsCheck{},
    &MayorStateValidCheck{},
}
```","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T16:37:05.267701-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T23:19:38.746608-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-f9x.5","depends_on_id":"gt-f9x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-15T16:37:05.268035-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-f9x.5","depends_on_id":"gt-f9x.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T16:37:34.289236-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-f9x.5","depends_on_id":"gt-f9x.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T16:37:34.380374-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-f9x.6","title":"Rig doctor checks: Refinery health, clones, gitignore","description":"Rig-level doctor checks.

## Checks

### RigIsGitRepo
- Verify .git/ directory exists
- Verify git status works
- Fix: Cannot auto-fix

### GitExcludeConfigured
- Check .git/info/exclude contains Gas Town dirs
- Required entries: polecats/ witness/ refinery/ mayor/
- Fix: Append missing entries

### WitnessExists
- Verify witness/ directory exists
- Verify witness/rig/ is a git clone
- Verify witness/mail/inbox.jsonl exists
- Fix: Create structure, clone repo

### RefineryExists
- Verify refinery/ directory exists
- Verify refinery/rig/ is a git clone
- Verify refinery/mail/inbox.jsonl exists
- Fix: Create structure, clone repo

### MayorCloneExists
- Verify mayor/ directory exists
- Verify mayor/rig/ is a git clone
- Fix: Create structure, clone repo

### PolecatClonesValid
- For each polecat in polecats/:
  - Verify is a git clone
  - Verify on polecat branch
  - Warn if has uncommitted changes
- Fix: Cannot auto-fix (data loss risk)

### BeadsConfigValid (if applicable)
- If .beads/ exists, verify bd commands work
- Check beads sync status
- Warn if out of sync
- Fix: Run bd sync

## Implementation

```go
var RigChecks = []Check{
    &RigIsGitRepoCheck{},
    &GitExcludeConfiguredCheck{},
    &WitnessExistsCheck{},
    &RefineryExistsCheck{},
    &MayorCloneExistsCheck{},
    &PolecatClonesValidCheck{},
    &BeadsConfigValidCheck{},
}
```","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T16:37:06.543281-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T21:18:54.012863-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-f9x.6","depends_on_id":"gt-f9x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-15T16:37:06.543796-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-f9x.6","depends_on_id":"gt-f9x.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T16:37:34.46868-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-f9x.7","title":"Connection interface: Protocol for local/remote ops","description":"Abstract interface for local vs remote (SSH) operations.

## Concept

Enable Gas Town to manage rigs on remote machines via SSH. The Connection interface abstracts whether operations happen locally or remotely.

## Interface

```go
type Connection interface {
    // Identification
    Name() string
    IsLocal() bool

    // File operations
    ReadFile(path string) ([]byte, error)
    WriteFile(path string, data []byte, perm os.FileMode) error
    MkdirAll(path string, perm os.FileMode) error
    Remove(path string) error
    Stat(path string) (os.FileInfo, error)
    Glob(pattern string) ([]string, error)

    // Command execution
    Exec(cmd string, args ...string) ([]byte, error)
    ExecDir(dir, cmd string, args ...string) ([]byte, error)

    // Tmux (for session management)
    TmuxNewSession(name, dir string) error
    TmuxKillSession(name string) error
    TmuxSendKeys(session, keys string) error
    TmuxCapturePane(session string, lines int) (string, error)
}
```

## Implementations

- LocalConnection (gt-f9x.8): Direct file/exec operations
- SSHConnection (future): Operations via SSH

## Usage

```go
func NewRigManager(conn Connection, ...) *RigManager

// Operations work the same regardless of connection type
rm.DiscoverRigs()  // uses conn.Glob, conn.ReadFile
```

## Design Notes

- Connection obtained from MachineRegistry (gt-f9x.9)
- Default is always local
- SSH connection requires machine config (host, key, user)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T16:37:07.764838-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T23:17:18.465919-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-f9x.7","depends_on_id":"gt-f9x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-15T16:37:07.765169-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-qh2","title":"Session cycling UX: smooth transitions via TUI wrapper","description":"## Problem

Current CLI agent session cycling is painful:
- Shell → CC starts → priming → context loads → ready → work → exit/crash → repeat
- Each cycle is 30-60 seconds of cold boot
- No continuity between shell and agent's inner state
- Raw "session not running, starting..." loop is the baseline

## GGT Advantages (already have)

- Beads: Work state survives session death completely
- Mail: Handoff notes from past-self to future-self  
- Prime commands: Structured context reload

## Gap: Transition Mechanics

Ideas to explore when actively using CLI:

1. **In-band cycling** - `/restart` or `/cycle` command, agent handles own restart without dropping to shell

2. **Hot standby** - TUI maintains pre-warmed session in background, switch to already-primed agent

3. **Persistent wrapper** - Bubbletea TUI stays running across session cycles, CC sessions come/go inside it

4. **Session pooling** - Keep 2-3 primed sessions ready, never wait for cold start

## Deferred

Deliberately P4 until we're actively using the simpler CLI and feel the pain firsthand.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T20:38:12.660716-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T23:17:34.27061-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-rm3","title":"CLI: gt refinery commands (start, stop, status, queue)","description":"CLI commands for managing the Refinery agent.

## Commands

```bash
gt refinery start <rig>      # Start refinery for a rig
gt refinery stop <rig>       # Stop refinery
gt refinery status <rig>     # Show refinery status
gt refinery queue <rig>      # Show merge queue
```

## gt refinery start

Starts the Refinery daemon for the specified rig.

Options:
- --foreground: Run in foreground (default: background)
- --auto-merge: Enable auto-merge (default: from config)

## gt refinery stop

Stops a running Refinery. Gracefully finishes current MR if processing.

## gt refinery status

Shows:
- Running state (running/stopped)
- Current MR being processed (if any)
- Queue length
- Last merge time
- Recent activity

## gt refinery queue

Shows the merge queue:
```
Merge queue for 'wyvern':
  1. [pending] Toast/polecat-auth-fix (15m ago)
  2. [pending] Capable/polecat-new-feature (5m ago)
  
1 merged today, 0 rejected
```

## Implementation

Uses gt-ov2 (Refinery agent) for daemon functionality.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:22:24.754361-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T14:16:34.593637-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T14:16:34.593637-08:00","close_reason":"Closed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-rm3","depends_on_id":"gt-ov2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:22:30.679909-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-sd6","title":"Enhanced polecat decommission prompting","description":"Add decommission checklist to polecat AGENTS.md.template. Make crystal clear: verify ALL before signaling done.

## Checklist for AGENTS.md.template

```markdown
## Decommission Checklist

**CRITICAL**: Before signaling done, you MUST complete this checklist.
The Witness will verify each item and bounce you back if dirty.

### Pre-Done Verification

```bash
# 1. Git status - must be clean
git status
# Expected: "nothing to commit, working tree clean"

# 2. Stash list - must be empty
git stash list
# Expected: (empty output)

# 3. Beads sync - must be up to date
bd sync --status
# Expected: "Up to date" or "Nothing to sync"

# 4. Branch merged - your work must be on main
git log main --oneline -1
git log HEAD --oneline -1
# Expected: Same commit
```

### If Any Check Fails

- **Uncommitted changes**: Commit them or discard if unnecessary
- **Stashes**: Pop and commit, or drop if obsolete
- **Beads out of sync**: Run `bd sync`
- **Branch not merged**: Complete the merge workflow

### Signaling Done

Only after ALL checks pass:

```bash
bd close <issue-id>
bd sync
town mail send <rig>/witness -s "Work Complete" -m "Issue <id> done."
```
```

## Implementation

Add to AGENTS.md.template in the polecat prompting section.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T19:48:57.911311-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T20:47:30.062333-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-sd6","depends_on_id":"gt-82y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T19:49:06.008061-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
